step_by_step_thinking:
When performing an emergency laparotomy for suspected intestinal obstruction, one should initially focus on visualizing the cecum. The presence or absence of dilated loops of ileal mucosa beyond the cecum can help differentiate between a high (small bowel) versus low (large bowel) obstruction. Answer Choice C: Cecum.
Answer: C Based on my clinical experience, visualization of the cecum allows for assessment of distension patterns that indicate if the obstruction involves primarily smaller intestines above this point or larger intestine below. This helps guide further management decisions. Therefore,
